title: [InstCombine] Missed optimization: Fold (sext(a) & c1) == c2 to (a & c3) == trunc(c2)

tests.outside_scope_reason: Tests i16->i64 sign extension pattern. Existing tests only cover i8->i32 extensions. This validates the optimization works across different bitwidth combinations not mentioned in the summary.

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i1 @i16_to_i64_sext_and_cmp(i16 %x) {
#0:
  %#1 = sext i16 %x to i64
  %#2 = and i64 %#1, -1
  %#3 = icmp eq i64 %#2, 5
  ret i1 %#3
}
=>
define i1 @i16_to_i64_sext_and_cmp(i16 %x) {
#0:
  %#1 = icmp eq i16 %x, 5
  ret i1 %#1
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.outside_scope_reason: Tests comparison against zero (C2=0). Existing tests use C2=1 or C2=2. Zero comparison is an important edge case not covered in the summary examples.

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i1 @sext_and_zero_comparison(i8 %x) {
#0:
  %#1 = sext i8 %x to i32
  %#2 = and i32 %#1, 255
  %#3 = icmp eq i32 %#2, 0
  ret i1 %#3
}
=>
define i1 @sext_and_zero_comparison(i8 %x) {
#0:
  %#1 = icmp eq i8 %x, 0
  ret i1 %#1
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.outside_scope_reason: Tests C1=-1 (all bits set including sign bit). While summary mentions sign bit handling, existing tests use C1=-2147483647 or 1073741826, not the full mask case which exercises the sign bit preservation logic differently.

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i1 @sext_full_mask_with_sign_bit(i8 %x) {
#0:
  %#1 = sext i8 %x to i32
  %#2 = and i32 %#1, 4294967295
  %#3 = icmp eq i32 %#2, 100
  ret i1 %#3
}
=>
define i1 @sext_full_mask_with_sign_bit(i8 %x) {
#0:
  %#1 = icmp eq i8 %x, 100
  ret i1 %#1
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
